Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results note anthocyanins are most sufficiently accumulated in petals.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Results/Discussion indicate FhMYB27 strongly represses anthocyanin and acts via C-terminal repression domains to convert MBW activator function into suppressive output.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Results support transcriptional repression via interaction with FhTT8L/MBW complex and feedback regulation by upstream activation of FhMYB27.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: ABGs including Fh3GT1 and the tested regulatory promoters are directly assayed in Results.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Core focus of Results is repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Expression rises during flower development, and promoter assays/ChIP support activation by FhPAP1/MBW context.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Yeast, Gal4:GUS interaction assays, and BiFC support interaction with FhTT8L.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Results conclude FhMYB27 acts through MBW complex by binding FhTT8L and converting activator complex into repressor complex.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same Freesia assays show grouping with LBGs/MBW components during development and high petal expression.
